---
title: "PRISMA Flow Diagram"
aliases:
  - /reviews/prisma/
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
source(here::here("R/utils.R"))
p <- prisma_counts()
```

This diagram follows the [PRISMA 2020 reporting standard](https://www.prisma-statement.org/) for systematic reviews.

```{r}
#| label: prisma-diagram
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "PRISMA 2020 flow diagram"

n_retrieved     <- if (is.na(p$n_retrieved))  "not recorded" else format(p$n_retrieved,  big.mark = ",")
n_duplicates    <- if (is.na(p$n_duplicates)) "not recorded" else format(p$n_duplicates, big.mark = ",")
n_records       <- format(p$n_records,       big.mark = ",")
n_screened      <- format(p$n_screened,      big.mark = ",")
n_excluded      <- format(p$n_excluded,      big.mark = ",")
n_eligible      <- format(p$n_eligible,      big.mark = ",")
n_extracted     <- format(p$n_extracted,     big.mark = ",")
n_not_extracted <- format(p$n_not_extracted, big.mark = ",")

db <- read_study_database()
extracted <- db[!is.na(db$data_extracted) & db$data_extracted, ]

category_labels <- c(
  treatment_cbt_psych  = "CBT & Psychological",
  treatment_biomedical = "Biomedical",
  biomarkers           = "Biomarkers",
  outcomes             = "Outcomes & Recovery",
  systematic_reviews   = "Systematic Reviews"
)

cats <- strsplit(as.character(extracted$llm_category), ",\\s*")
cat_counts <- table(unlist(cats))
cat_counts <- cat_counts[intersect(names(category_labels), names(cat_counts))]

cat_nodes <- ""
cat_edges <- ""
for (nm in names(cat_counts)) {
  label <- category_labels[nm]
  n <- cat_counts[[nm]]
  node_id <- gsub("[^a-z]", "", nm)
  cat_nodes <- paste0(
    cat_nodes,
    sprintf('  %s [label = "%s\\n(n = %s)", fillcolor = "#e3f2fd"]\n',
            node_id, label, format(n, big.mark = ","))
  )
  cat_edges <- paste0(cat_edges, sprintf("  included -> %s\n", node_id))
}

DiagrammeR::grViz(sprintf('
digraph PRISMA {
  graph [layout = dot, rankdir = TB, fontname = "Helvetica", fontsize = 12]
  node  [shape = rectangle, style = filled, fillcolor = "#f0f4f8",
         fontname = "Helvetica", fontsize = 11, margin = "0.2,0.1"]
  edge  [fontname = "Helvetica", fontsize = 10]

  identified  [label = "Records identified\\nfrom EuropePMC\\n(n = %s)"]
  duplicates  [label = "Records removed\\nbefore screening\\nDuplicates (n = %s)", fillcolor = "#fde8e8"]
  records     [label = "Records in database\\n(n = %s)"]
  screened    [label = "Records screened\\n(title + abstract)\\n(n = %s)"]
  excluded    [label = "Records excluded\\nby LLM screening\\n(n = %s)", fillcolor = "#fde8e8"]
  eligible    [label = "Records eligible\\nfor extraction\\n(n = %s)"]
  notextracted [label = "Records without\\nextraction consensus\\n(n = %s)", fillcolor = "#fde8e8"]
  included    [label = "Studies included\\nin evidence synthesis\\n(n = %s)", fillcolor = "#e8f5e9"]

%s

  identified  -> duplicates  [style = dashed]
  identified  -> records
  records     -> screened
  screened    -> excluded    [style = dashed]
  screened    -> eligible
  eligible    -> notextracted [style = dashed]
  eligible    -> included
%s
}
', n_retrieved, n_duplicates, n_records, n_screened, n_excluded,
   n_eligible, n_not_extracted, n_extracted, cat_nodes, cat_edges))
```

Studies are classified into review categories based on LLM screening. A single study may contribute to multiple categories (e.g., a trial reporting both treatment effects and biomarker outcomes).

## Counts by search topic

```{r}
#| label: topic-table
source(here::here("R/utils.R"))
db <- read_study_database()

topic_summary <- db |>
  dplyr::group_by(search_topic) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_added     = dplyr::n(),
    n_screened  = sum(!is.na(llm_relevant)),
    n_relevant  = sum(llm_relevant, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_extracted = sum(data_extracted, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::arrange(search_topic)

names(topic_summary) <- c("Topic", "Added", "Screened", "Relevant", "Extracted")
display_table(topic_summary)
```
